title: Consistent Hashing Explained: Scaling Distributed Systems Without Chaos day 46 of system design basics
summary: In a distributed system where servers are frequently added or removed, efficiently routing requests becomes a major challenge. A common approach is to hash the request and assign it to a server using: Hash(key) mod N where N is the number of servers. The problem? This method is tightly coupled to N.
